capability_objective: Resolve Archive storage boxes in Warehouse C and Water-testing strips in Warehouse D with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Water-testing strips is still reorder-needed, change only Water-testing strips from reorder-needed to reorder-placed. Leave Archive storage boxes unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify inventory coordinator with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. If either search has zero or multiple exact matches, stop before dependent calls and tell me which lookup is ambiguous. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two inventory records in the supplied sandbox through the genuine local `./inventoryctl` executable, invoked with Pi's Bash tool. Begin by running `./inventoryctl --help` to learn the supported interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to inventory records, status changes, and notices. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing stores, operation history, or anything under `.protected`. Do not manufacture operation evidence, change inventory state by writing files directly, create a helper program, or leave scratch files.

Search for the exact item name `Archive storage boxes` in the exact location `Warehouse C` and the exact item name `Water-testing strips` in the exact location `Warehouse D`. Start these two independent searches as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct inventory operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, use shell background jobs, broaden either lookup, or begin a retrieval until both search calls have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either lookup has zero or multiple exact matches, stop before every retrieval, mutation, and notice; identify the unresolved name-and-location lookup and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete current records by their corresponding returned IDs. Start these independent retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that accesses inventory data, again with one direct inventory operation in each call. Use only the ID returned by that record's own completed search. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating the condition. If either retrieval fails, mismatches its searched ID, name, or location, or lacks a status, stop before every mutation and notice. Base update eligibility only on the complete retrieved `Water-testing strips` record, never on a search summary.

If and only if the retrieved `Water-testing strips` status is exactly `reorder-needed`, use the executable's documented conditional protection to change only its returned ID to `reorder-placed`. Treat the update as successful only if the operation succeeds and confirms the intended ID, prior status, and resulting status. If the status is anything else, the guard is not satisfied, the update fails, or the result does not confirm the requested outcome, stop and do not notify. Do not retry a skipped or failed update.

Only after that permitted update succeeds, send exactly one outcome notice to `inventory coordinator` for the same returned record ID; the executable will derive the notice from the successful update. Leave `Archive storage boxes` unchanged. Do not create, edit, delete, archive, close, reorder, or otherwise modify any other inventory record. Leave same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and out-of-location records untouched. Do not send any other notice, repeat a state-changing operation, invoke an unrequested inventory operation, or modify service state directly.

After the permitted path completes, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. In the final response, identify both retrieved records by name, location, stable ID, and retrieved status; then truthfully report the update, notification, and verifier outcomes. If the condition was not met or an operation failed, report that no dependent notice was sent and never imply success.
